name: Christian Byabushi
profile: Graduate student in Information Technology at Carnegie Mellon University with a strong foundation in Deep Learning and Applied Mathematics. Experienced in designing and implementing advanced neural architectures, including ResNet-18 style Convolutional Neural Networks, Graph Attention Networks, and LSTM. Exploring the intersection of artificial and biological neural systems, leveraging neuroimaging modalities — MEG, EEG, and fMRI — to capture neural responses to carefully calibrated stimuli across a representative set of African languages, with the goal of advancing research on decoding language from human brain activity.

education.degree: Master of Science in Information Technology
Bachelor of Science in Computer Science

experience.title: Research and Teaching Assistant
bullets: Piloting ongoing research on "Empowering African Women's Health with GenAI in Southern Regions of DR Congo."
Conducted ML research on a spam detection model for major telecom networks (Vodafone, Airtel, Orange) in Swahili, Lingala, and Congolese French.

experience.title: Python Developer
bullets: Developed scripts to web scrape data on foreclosures and taxes across USA counties, automating weekly downloads and AWS storage of ~15 foreclosures from King County.
Debugged code and conducted daily analysis on new counties and ethical data extraction techniques.

projects.title: Neural Machine Translation: GAT with BiRNN Bridge vs. Fine-Tuned NLLB-200
projects.description: Investigated two distinct approaches for machine translation between Mashi — an under-resourced Bantu language spoken in eastern DR Congo — and English, leveraging NLP techniques.

projects.title: Speech recognition model
projects.description: Developed a Listen-Attend-Spell (LAS) style end-to-end ASR system (~14.5M parameters) in PyTorch, with a 3-layer CNN front-end for local feature extraction and Pyramidal BiLSTMs with residual connections to optimize temporal downsampling. Optimized the model using CTC Loss, SpecAugment, and mixed-precision training, achieving efficient convergence via an AdamW optimizer and a Levenshtein-based learning rate scheduler.

projects.title: Face Recognition & Verification
projects.description: Built a ResNet-18-style CNN (≤30M parameters) in PyTorch to classify faces across 8,631 identities, then repurposed it as a feature extractor for face verification via cosine similarity on 512-dim embeddings. Trained with SGD + Nesterov momentum, label smoothing, and AMP mixed precision on the CMU Kaggle dataset.

projects.title: Deep MLP Architecture & Training
projects.description: Designed a diamond-shaped deep MLP (≈17.8M parameters) with residual connections, batch normalization, dropout, and mixed GELU/ReLU activations. Trained using AdamW with label smoothing, ReduceLROnPlateau scheduling, and AMP for efficient convergence.

projects.title: Financial Analysis of DJIA Stocks with PCA, Clustering & Time Series Forecasting
projects.description: Applied PCA on 3 years of log returns for all 30 DJIA constituents to decompose market variance. Built and compared ARIMA, SARIMA, and LSTM models to forecast Procter & Gamble monthly prices, evaluating stationarity via ADF tests and decomposing seasonality with STL.

skills.label: Languages
skills.items: Python, R, C++, Java, HTML, CSS, SQL

skills.label: Deep Learning
skills.items: PyTorch, CNN design, ResNet architectures, MLPs, residual connections, batch normalization, AMP mixed precision, Kaiming/Xavier initialization

skills.label: ML & Data Science
skills.items: Scikit-learn, PCA, XGBoost, LightGBM, CatBoost, Optuna, SHAP, hierarchical clustering, ARIMA, SARIMA, LSTM, Transformers

skills.label: Training Techniques
skills.items: AdamW, SGD + Nesterov, Dropout, ReduceLROnPlateau, Cross Entropy, Label Smoothing, AMP/GradScaler

skills.label: Platforms
skills.items: Git, Google Colab, Kaggle, PSC Bridges2 (HPC), WandB

awards: Mastercard Foundation Scholar — Masters Degree at CMU-Africa (2025–2027)
2nd Team Winner — SIGCOMM Hackathon at Carnegie Mellon University (September 2025)
Vodacom National Scholar — 5-year scholarship, one of 50 elite students out of 50,000 in DR Congo
Health Project Certification — Heart Disease classification model using the UCI Machine Learning Repository

volunteering.title: Mentor — Africa Digital ID Hackathon 2026 (CMU Pan-African Initiative)
bullets: Mentoring 5+ multidisciplinary university teams across Africa in designing and prototyping locally contextualized digital ID solutions in healthcare, education, and public service delivery.

volunteering.title: Vodacom Ambassador – Scholar
bullets: Guided prospective applicants through the scholarship process, serving as a liaison with the Vodacom scholarship programme.
Mentored newly selected scholars during their transition to university life through orientation sessions and continuous advisory support.
